mcqs.question: In statistical inference, what is the primary purpose of simple random sampling of attributes?

options.text: To ensure every member of the population has an equal chance of being selected.
To deliberately select a sample that matches the population on key characteristics.
To reduce the cost and time associated with data collection.
To guarantee that the sample is perfectly representative of the population.

mcqs.explanation: Simple random sampling is defined by its core principle: every possible subset of a given size has the same probability of being chosen, which means every individual has an equal chance of selection.

mcqs.question: A university wants to estimate the proportion of students who own a laptop. They assign a number to every student on the campus roster and use a random number generator to select 200 students for a survey. What is this sampling method called?

options.text: Simple Random Sampling
Stratified Sampling
Cluster Sampling
Systematic Sampling

mcqs.explanation: This is a classic example of simple random sampling. The sampling frame (the roster) is defined, and individuals are selected purely by a random mechanism, giving everyone an equal probability of selection.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a key disadvantage of using simple random sampling for a very large and geographically dispersed population?

options.text: It requires a complete and accurate sampling frame, which can be difficult or expensive to obtain.
It often leads to a higher sampling error compared to other methods.
It introduces a significant amount of selection bias into the study.
It systematically under-represents minority groups within the population.

mcqs.explanation: The primary practical disadvantage of simple random sampling is the need for a complete list of every member of the population (the sampling frame), which may not exist or be accessible for large, spread-out groups.

mcqs.question: The sample proportion p̂ is an unbiased estimator of the population proportion p. What does this mean?

options.text: The value of p̂ will always be exactly equal to p.
The average of p̂ values from all possible samples is equal to p.
p̂ is guaranteed to be closer to p than any other estimator.
The sampling distribution of p̂ is always normally distributed.

mcqs.explanation: An unbiased estimator means that its expected value (the mean of its sampling distribution) is equal to the true population parameter. It does not mean it is always correct for a single sample, just that there is no systematic tendency to over- or under-estimate.

mcqs.question: What is the formula for the standard error (S.E.) of the sample proportion p̂?
mcqs.explanation: The standard error quantifies the variability of the sampling distribution. For a proportion, it is calculated as the square root of [p(1-p) divided by the sample size n].
